# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:3.8-slim as base

# Set environment variables
ENV PG_USER=postgres
ENV PG_PASSWORD=password
ENV REDIS_HOST=localhost
ENV API_HOST=localhost

RUN mkdir -p /app/services && mkdir -p /app/services/db

# Install dependencies for web service
WORKDIR /app/services/web
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt

# Build and run web service
WORKDIR /app/services/web
COPY . .

EXPOSE 80
CMD ["gunicorn", "--workers", "3", "-w", "main:app"]

# Install dependencies for API server
WORKDIR /app/services/api
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt

# Build and run API server
WORKDIR /app/services/api
COPY . .

EXPOSE 8080
CMD ["uvicorn", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8080", "main:app"]

# Install dependencies for database
WORKDIR /app/services/db
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt

# Build and run database container
WORKDIR /app/services/db
COPY . .

ENV DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:password@localhost:5432/dev
CMD ["flask", "run", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "5000"]

# Install Redis server dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y redis-server

EXPOSE 6379
CMD ["redis-server"]

# Expose ports
EXPOSE 80
EXPOSE 8080
EXPOSE 5432
EXPOSE 6379
